title: 10 medicinal constituents of Eurycoma longifolia (tongkat ali)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Eurycoma longifolia quassin is a plant-based compound extracted from the roots of Eurycoma longifolia, a flowering plant native to Southeast Asia. Quassin has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various ailments, including fever, malaria, and digestive issues. It is also believed to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a popular ingredient in health supplements.

Eurycoma longifolia eurycomanone is a bioactive compound extracted from the roots of this tropical plant. It has been traditionally used in Malaysian folk medicine to enhance physical strength and vitality, as well as treat various ailments including fever, rheumatism, and snake bites. Eurycomanone has also been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a promising ingredient for potential therapeutic applications.

Eurycoma longifolia eurycolactone is a bioactive compound isolated from the root of this plant. It has been shown to possess various pharmacological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cytotoxic properties. Eurycolactone has been reported to exhibit inhibitory effects on cancer cell proliferation, induce apoptosis, and reduce oxidative stress in cells. Its potential applications include the development of new treatments for cancer and other diseases related to oxidative stress.

Eurycoma longifolia lupeol is a bioactive compound found in the root of this plant. It has been traditionally used in traditional medicine to enhance male fertility and sexual function. Lupeol has also been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a potential therapeutic agent for various diseases. Additionally, studies have demonstrated its ability to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and induce apoptosis in certain types of tumors.

Eurycoma longifolia ursolic acid is a bioactive compound extracted from the root of this plant species. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to enhance male fertility and vitality. Ursolic acid has been shown to possess potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects. Its potential benefits include improved sperm quality, enhanced libido, and overall reproductive health.

Eurycoma longifolia beta-amyrin is a type of triterpenoid saponin that is extracted from the plant's roots and stems. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for centuries to treat various health issues, including fever, malaria, and respiratory problems. Beta-amyrin has also been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, making it a potential natural remedy for several modern diseases.

Eurycoma longifolia oleane is a plant-based extract derived from the roots of the Eurycoma longifolia species. Native to Southeast Asia, it has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to improve male fertility and vitality. The oleane extract is believed to enhance sexual function, boost energy levels, and support immune system function. Its popularity has led to widespread use among athletes and individuals seeking to improve overall well-being.

Eurycoma longifolia oleanolic acid is a bioactive compound extracted from the plant's root and leaf. It has been traditionally used in Asian medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Oleanolic acid has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, making it a potential therapeutic agent for various diseases, including cancer, cardiovascular disease, and neurodegenerative disorders.

Eurycoma longifolia urs-12-en-28-oic acid is a triterpenoid compound extracted from the roots of Eurycoma longifolia, a plant native to Southeast Asia. This compound has been found to possess various bioactive properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antidiabetic activities. Urs-12-en-28-oic acid has also been reported to have potential therapeutic effects on sexual function, stress relief, and muscle recovery.

Eurycoma longifolia betulinic acid is a unique compound extracted from the root of the plant. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to enhance vitality and sexual function. Research suggests that betulinic acid exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a promising candidate for the treatment of various diseases, including cancer and infectious disorders.

intro: Eurycoma longifolia has active constituents such as quassinoids, alkaloids, and flavonoids. These compounds possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. For example, the quassinoids can help improve sexual function and boost testosterone levels, leading to enhanced libido and fertility in men. Additionally, the antioxidants may help protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer, ultimately improving overall well-being and quality of life.
intro_desc: Eurycoma longifolia has active constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides. These constituents confer medicinal properties such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and testosterone-boosting effects. The antioxidant properties can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress, while the anti-inflammatory properties can alleviate symptoms of pain and inflammation. The testosterone-boosting effect can improve muscle mass, strength, and fertility, thereby enhancing physical performance and quality of life.
